{
  "decision": "fail",

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Wrong wirenut size staged for termination work; improper connector size can lead to high-resistance connection, overheating, and fire."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Replace staged wirenuts with connectors correctly sized for conductor count and gauge per manufacturer's instructions and NEC 110.14(B).",
    "Verify conductor material (Cu/Al) and select listed connectors suitable for the combination.",
    "Ensure connectors are tightened to manufacturer's specified torque values."
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC 110.14(B) requires splicing devices and connectors to be used in accordance with their listing and be suitable for the conductor material, size, and number. Using an undersized or oversized wirenut violates this requirement before work begins, introducing latent failure risk.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14",
    "NEC 110.14(B)"
  ]
}
